original_hypothesis.title: Incentives Mapping Not Zeroed After transferIncentives Causes Irrecoverable Funds Display
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: After transferIncentives() sends incentive tokens to the external bribe, the incentives mapping retains non-zero values. If the pool later transitions to NOT_QUALIFIED (via setPoolStatus), claimIncentives() will attempt to transfer tokens the pool no longer holds, reverting via SafeERC20 and permanently preventing any incentive claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: transferIncentives() approves and sends tokens to external_bribe but never sets incentives[token] = 0. The _setPoolStatus function allows transitioning to NOT_QUALIFIED from any state when called by manager. In NOT_QUALIFIED state, claimIncentives() iterates incentiveTokens and tries to transfer incentives[token] amounts, but the balance is zero, causing permanent revert.

proof_of_signal.pattern: transferIncentives sends tokens via notifyRewardAmount but never zeros incentives[token]

preconditions: Manager calls setPoolStatus(NOT_QUALIFIED) after incentive transfer

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: incentives[token] never zeroed in transferIncentives; claimIncentives reads stale non-zero values
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Token owner sees claimable incentives but transfer reverts due to zero balance
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: transferIncentives should set incentives[incentiveTokens[i]] = 0 after sending

analysis_questions: Can manager set pool to NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH?
Is there any path to recover incentive tokens from external bribe?
validation_requirements: Verify claimIncentives reverts when balance is zero
Check if external bribe has recovery mechanism

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Token owner adds incentives. Pool progresses to PRE_LAUNCH, incentives sent to bribe. Manager sets NOT_QUALIFIED (e.g., external issue). Token owner calls claimIncentives, transaction reverts because pool has no incentive token balance. Incentive tokens are unrecoverable.
prerequisites: Pool transitions back to NOT_QUALIFIED after incentive transfer

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Token owner permanently loses all incentive tokens deposited

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for claim after transfer + status change

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: N/A — affects token owner's deposited incentives
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Token owner discovers when trying to claim

validation_result.impact_summary: transferIncentives sends tokens to external bribe but never zeros incentives[token], causing stale state in view functions and potential permanent DoS on claimIncentives if pool reaches NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The transferIncentives function (GenesisPool.sol:138-153) transfers incentive tokens to the external bribe via notifyRewardAmount but never sets incentives[incentiveTokens[i]] = 0. This is a clear code defect — the accounting state (incentives mapping) diverges from the actual token balance held by the contract.

After transferIncentives executes:
1. getIncentivesInfo() returns stale non-zero amounts for tokens the contract no longer holds
2. claimableIncentives() reports claimable amounts that are actually zero in balance
3. If the pool ever reaches NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH, claimIncentives() would try to safeTransfer tokens the contract doesn't hold, permanently reverting

Regarding reachability of the full DoS scenario: In the current GenesisPoolManager code, checkBeforeEpochFlip only sets NOT_QUALIFIED from PRE_LISTING state (before incentives are transferred). The transition PRE_LAUNCH → NOT_QUALIFIED is not triggered by any existing manager function. However, the GenesisPool.setPoolStatus() function has zero transition validation — it accepts any status from any current status. Combined with the upgradeable nature of GenesisPoolManager, this path could become reachable. The code defect (missing cleanup) exists regardless of current reachability.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is accurate. Lines 138-153 of GenesisPool.sol show transferIncentives sending tokens without zeroing the mapping. Lines 226-238 show claimIncentives reading the stale mapping values and attempting transfers. The defect is confirmed by code inspection.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: After sending each incentive token to external_bribe via notifyRewardAmount, the function never sets incentives[incentiveTokens[i]] = 0. The tokens leave the contract but the accounting mapping retains non-zero values, creating a permanent state inconsistency.

Reads _amount from the stale incentives mapping. If transferIncentives was previously called, _amount is non-zero but the contract balance is zero, causing safeTransfer to revert. The transfer is unconditional (no _amount > 0 check), so even zero amounts are transferred, but non-zero stale values will cause revert.

No validation of state transitions. Any status can be set from any current status. This enables the transition from PRE_LAUNCH to NOT_QUALIFIED if the manager ever calls setPoolStatus(NOT_QUALIFIED) from a post-incentive-transfer state.

calling_sequence: 1. tokenOwner calls addIncentives() → tokens transferred to GenesisPool, incentives[token] set to non-zero, incentiveTokens array populated
2. depositToken threshold met → manager calls _preLaunchPool → transferIncentives() called
3. transferIncentives sends tokens to external_bribe via notifyRewardAmount, sets poolStatus = PRE_LAUNCH, but does NOT zero incentives mapping
4. getIncentivesInfo() and claimableIncentives() now return stale non-zero values
5. If setPoolStatus(NOT_QUALIFIED) is called (no transition validation prevents this), claimIncentives() reads stale amounts and reverts on safeTransfer
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defect is in step 3: transferIncentives (GenesisPool.sol:138-153) loops through incentiveTokens, reads incentives[incentiveTokens[i]], approves and calls notifyRewardAmount to send tokens to external_bribe, but never executes incentives[incentiveTokens[i]] = 0. Post-transfer, the contract holds zero balance of these tokens but the mapping reports non-zero. View functions getIncentivesInfo() (line 287-295) and claimableIncentives() (line 215-224) return incorrect data. If NOT_QUALIFIED is reached, claimIncentives() (line 226-238) attempts safeTransfer with stale _amount against zero balance, permanently reverting.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for GenesisPool or GenesisPoolManager initialization relevant to this hypothesis.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The full DoS scenario requires the pool to reach NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH. In the current GenesisPoolManager code, checkBeforeEpochFlip only disqualifies pools from PRE_LISTING state (before transferIncentives). From PRE_LAUNCH, it transitions to PRE_LAUNCH_DEPOSIT_DISABLED, then to LAUNCH. So the current manager code doesn't directly trigger the full attack path. However: (1) the missing cleanup is an objective code defect, (2) setPoolStatus has no transition validation enabling any transition, (3) the manager is upgradeable so future code could trigger this path, (4) view functions already return incorrect data. The code defect exists regardless of current exploitation path availability.
validation_result.impact_assessment: PRIMARY: stale incentives mapping causes incorrect view function outputs (getIncentivesInfo, claimableIncentives) for all pools that had incentives transferred. SECONDARY: if the pool ever reaches NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH (possible via manager upgrade or future functionality), claimIncentives permanently reverts, locking incentive token recovery. The token owner's incentive tokens sent to external_bribe become irrecoverable in the NOT_QUALIFIED scenario.
recommended_mitigations: In transferIncentives, add incentives[incentiveTokens[i]] = 0 after each successful notifyRewardAmount call
Add state transition validation in _setPoolStatus to enforce valid lifecycle transitions
Add a balance check or _amount > 0 guard in claimIncentives before calling safeTransfer
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy GenesisPool with mock tokens and manager. 2. Call addIncentives with a test token. 3. Call transferIncentives (via manager) to send to mock external_bribe. 4. Verify incentives[token] is still non-zero via getIncentivesInfo(). 5. Call setPoolStatus(NOT_QUALIFIED) via manager. 6. Call claimIncentives as tokenOwner and observe revert due to insufficient balance.
uncertainties: Whether the current manager code ever triggers NOT_QUALIFIED after PRE_LAUNCH (analysis suggests it does not in current code, but setPoolStatus has no transition guards)
Whether the manager contract could be upgraded to add such a path
Whether the external_bribe has any recovery mechanism for tokens sent via notifyRewardAmount
additional_testing_needed: Test claimIncentives after transferIncentives + manual setPoolStatus(NOT_QUALIFIED)
Verify getIncentivesInfo returns stale data after transferIncentives
Test whether all manager upgrade paths could enable the NOT_QUALIFIED transition from PRE_LAUNCH
